editorLockedDescription: Run the starter program once to unlock editing. Until then, the starter program runs drop_ball() for you.
editorUnlockedDescription: Write one small strategy at a time. A good first step is set_aim(bonus_lane) and then drop_ball().
helperEditorLockedDescription: The helper editor unlocks in the functions topic.
helperEditorUnlockedDescription: Store one reusable function here and call it from the main program.
programReadyMessage: Valid program. This run will launch {count} ball(s).
programZeroStepMessage: Valid code, but this run launches 0 balls. Add a drop or change the logic.
runBlockedInvalidProgram: Fix the program before running it again.
runPanelRunningMessage: The machine is resolving the current run.
programErrorEmpty: Your program is empty. Add at least one drop_ball().
programErrorTooManyLines: Too many non-empty lines. The current limit is {limit}.
programErrorInvalidLine: Line {line}: this line is not valid right now.
programErrorUnsupportedForLoop: Line {line}: only for _ in range(N): is supported right now.
programErrorForRangeLimit: Line {line}: range() must stay between 1 and {max}.
programErrorForBodyRequired: Line {line}: add at least one indented line under this block.
programErrorInvalidForBody: Line {line}: this line is not allowed inside the current loop body.
programErrorNestedBlocks: Line {line}: nested blocks are not supported in this prototype.
programErrorUnexpectedIndentation: Line {line}: this indented line needs a supported block above it.
programErrorStepLimitExceeded: This program would launch too many balls at once. The current limit is {max}.
programErrorInvalidExpression: Line {line}: this expression is not valid.
programErrorInvalidCondition: Line {line}: this condition is not valid.
programErrorInvalidSetAim: Line {line}: set_aim(...) needs one numeric expression.
programErrorAimRangeLimit: Line {line}: set_aim(...) must stay between 1 and 3.
programErrorInvalidFunctionDefinition: Line {line}: helper functions must use def name(): in the helper editor.
programErrorHelperLimitExceeded: You can define up to {limit} helper function right now.
programErrorHelperLineLimitExceeded: The helper editor can use up to {limit} non-empty lines right now.
programErrorHelperNotDefined: Line {line}: helper function {name} does not exist yet.
availableStructuresEmpty: No Python structures are unlocked yet.
availableSyntaxStepLimit: Up to {limit} balls can be launched in one run.
referenceNoExamples: Examples appear here as new topics unlock.
referenceBonusLaneDescription: Which lane is boosted right now.
referenceJackpotSideDescription: Which side rail currently gives the jackpot bonus.
referenceReturnSideDescription: The side that can cash in after the jackpot side opens the gate.
referenceReturnGateDescription: 1 means the return gate is open, 0 means it is closed.
referenceFeederChargeDescription: How many combo lights are currently filled.
referenceComboTargetDescription: Which lane is currently marked as the combo target.
referenceBurstReadyDescription: 1 means lightning is ready, 0 means the feeder is still charging.
nextStepOnboardingBody: Run the starter program once to see how one ball moves through the machine. After that, editing unlocks.
nextStepCheckpointReadyBody: {topic} is ready for a short checkpoint. Pass the 3 tasks to unlock the next topic.
nextStepCheckpointActiveBody: Finish the current {topic} checkpoint task to keep progressing.
nextStepCompletedBody: The first guided topics are complete. Keep experimenting, buying support upgrades, and polishing your strategy.
helpCenterEmpty: The latest guides will appear here.
guideWelcomeBody: The opening run is only here to show the board. drop_ball() launches one ball, and you do not need to answer any tasks before you see it happen.
guideVariablesUnlockBody: You can now edit the main program. Lane numbers are simple: 1 = left, 2 = center, 3 = right. bonus_lane tells you which lane is boosted right now, so a great first program is set_aim(bonus_lane) and then drop_ball().
guideConditionsUnlockBody: The board now shows a jackpot rail and a return rail. jackpot_side tells you which rail opens the gate, and return_side shows where that gate can be cashed in.
guideFunctionsUnlockBody: You now have a helper editor for one reusable tactic. A good helper call can arm a visible echo target on the board for the next aligned shot.
guideLoopsUnlockBody: Loops now let one good tactic repeat on purpose. Watch the combo lights fill, then use for _ in range(n): to build a short planned burst.
currentGoalBoardMeaningLabel: Why it matters on the board
currentGoalOnboardingGoal: Watch one safe run, understand the basic board, and unlock the editor.
currentGoalOnboardingAction: Run the starter program once, then clear the onboarding checks.
currentGoalCompletedGoal: The first four course modules are installed. Keep refining your strategy or expand the next semester topic.
currentGoalCompletedAction: Experiment with the full machine and polish how the modules work together.
currentGoalCompletedBoardMeaning: Progress now comes from calculated aim, branching logic, reusable tactics, and repeated bursts working as one machine.
currentGoalCheckpointAction: Start the checkpoint. Passing all 3 tasks installs {unlock} immediately.
currentGoalCheckpointActiveAction: Finish the open checkpoint task. Passing all 3 tasks installs {unlock} immediately.
currentGoalSpotlightAction: Try the new construct once, or dismiss this spotlight after you understand the snippet.
boardSubtitle: Bonus lane is always visible. New rails, targets, and combo lights appear here as you unlock more code tools.
boardLegendBonusHelp: Bonus lane = the lane that is boosted right now.
activityFeedEmpty: Unlocks, solved tasks, and support upgrades will appear here.
shopSubtitle: Spend score on already learned modules. Score improves syntax you own; it never unlocks syntax by itself.
shopAvailableHint: Earn a few more points to buy this support upgrade.
